title: Where Named Things Learn to Move

features.name: the-voice-channel
features.desc-on-touch: An archway through which wind passes, carrying words you recognize as speech but feel as motion. The air itself is talking.

features.name: the-word-pool-surface
features.desc-on-touch: Water that ripples with names, each word-ripple bending the light. Speech becomes visible only as it disturbs the still.

features.name: the-tower-of-shaped-wind
features.desc-on-touch: A stack of visible air-currents, each one a voice that learned to hold its shape. The tower sways but does not fall.

features.name: the-scatter-of-unheard-voices
features.desc-on-touch: Points of light scattered like dropped words, each one a name that shaped the world and then dissolved into static.

features.name: the-frequency-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: Unreadable writing that modulates as you listen to it. The inscription is the sound; the sound is the meaning.

inscription: the drain gives nothing voice; the voice gives the drain a reason to have been listening all along—and now the wind remembers what it spoke.

file.name: Wind_Drift_Vocal_Recordings_Unmixed.mp3
file.note: voices that learned to move the air, then the air that learned to move the voices—feedback until the boundary dissolved

exits.label: back down to where words still search for speakers
into the storm's own archive of speech
upward where the wind becomes language, language becomes current
